baseline.name: MemberServer
baseline.description: Posture profile for domain-joined or centrally managed Windows member servers.
sourceBasis: Microsoft Security Compliance Toolkit and current Microsoft Windows security guidance
Operational security telemetry and recoverability practices
notes: Application roles can require documented exceptions. The profile does not infer application-specific firewall, service, or authentication requirements.

summary.ScoringModel: Severity-weighted pass percentage. Exceptions, unknowns, warnings, and errors receive no pass credit; not-applicable and informational controls are excluded.

results.title: Built-in Guest account is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: An enabled guest account provides an unnecessary logon path with weak accountability.
results.remediation: Disable the built-in Guest account through Group Policy, Intune, or local security policy and verify that no application depends on it.

results.title: User Account Control is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Disabling UAC removes an important privilege boundary and weakens multiple Windows security features.
results.remediation: Set "User Account Control: Run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode" to Enabled and restart the device.

results.title: Administrator elevation prompts require consent
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Silent elevation makes malicious or accidental privileged execution harder to detect and contain.
results.remediation: Configure the administrator elevation prompt to "Prompt for consent for non-Windows binaries" or a stricter credential prompt.

results.title: Built-in Administrator uses Admin Approval Mode
results.message: The built-in Administrator account is not running in Admin Approval Mode.
results.rationale: The built-in Administrator otherwise runs with a full token without UAC consent.
results.remediation: Set "User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account" to Enabled.

results.title: LSA protection is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: LSA protection raises the bar for credential theft and blocks untrusted code from loading into LSASS.
results.remediation: Enable "Configure LSASS to run as a protected process" in policy. Test authentication and security software compatibility before broad enforcement.

results.title: WDigest credential caching is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Enabling WDigest credential caching can expose plaintext credentials to memory-access attacks.
results.remediation: Set UseLogonCredential to 0 under the WDigest security provider policy. A missing value is treated as the secure modern default.

results.title: Credential Guard is running
results.message: The Device Guard WMI provider did not return Credential Guard state.
results.rationale: Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets from the normal operating system.
results.remediation: Enable virtualization-based security and Credential Guard with the deployment mode appropriate for the device. Validate VPN, Wi-Fi, delegation, and legacy authentication dependencies first.

results.title: Windows LAPS password backup is configured
results.expected: Microsoft Entra ID or Active Directory
results.actual: Microsoft Entra ID or Active Directory
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Unique, rotated local administrator passwords reduce lateral movement and shared-password risk.
results.remediation: Configure Windows LAPS BackupDirectory through Intune CSP or Group Policy and validate permissions to retrieve passwords.

results.title: Windows LAPS password age is 30 days or less
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Regular rotation limits the useful lifetime of a disclosed local administrator password.
results.remediation: Configure PasswordAgeDays to 30 or fewer days, balancing rotation with operational recovery requirements.

results.title: Windows LAPS AD password encryption is enabled
results.message: Windows LAPS is backed up to Active Directory, but password encryption is disabled.
results.rationale: AD password encryption narrows exposure of stored LAPS secrets and enables stronger authorization controls.
results.remediation: Enable ADPasswordEncryptionEnabled for Active Directory-backed Windows LAPS after confirming the domain functional level and authorized decryptors.

results.title: Anonymous SAM enumeration is restricted
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Anonymous enumeration can disclose account information useful for reconnaissance.
results.remediation: Set "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts" to Enabled.

results.title: Anonymous access is restricted
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Restricting anonymous access reduces unauthenticated information disclosure and legacy null-session behavior.
results.remediation: Set "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" to Enabled where compatible.

results.title: Windows Firewall is enabled for every profile
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Host firewalls limit lateral movement and provide protection when network controls are absent or bypassed.
results.remediation: Enable Windows Firewall for all profiles and deploy explicit allow rules for required management and application traffic.

results.title: Default inbound firewall action is Block
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: A default-deny inbound posture limits unplanned exposure when no explicit rule exists.
results.remediation: Set the default inbound action to Block for Domain, Private, and Public profiles, then maintain explicit allow rules.

results.title: SMBv1 server component is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: SMBv1 is obsolete and lacks modern security protections.
results.remediation: Remove or disable the SMB 1.0/CIFS server component after identifying legacy dependencies.

results.title: SMBv1 client component is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: An SMBv1 client can be coerced into using an obsolete protocol against malicious or legacy servers.
results.remediation: Remove or disable the SMB 1.0/CIFS client component after identifying legacy dependencies.

results.title: SMB server signing is required
results.message: SMB server signing is required.
results.rationale: Required signing helps protect SMB traffic from tampering and relay attacks.
results.remediation: Require SMB server signing through Group Policy or the SMB server configuration. Validate legacy clients before rollout.

results.title: SMB client signing is required
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Required client signing helps prevent downgrade and relay scenarios when connecting to file services.
results.remediation: Require SMB client signing through Group Policy or SMB client configuration. Validate legacy appliances first.

results.title: Insecure SMB guest authentication is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Guest SMB authentication removes identity assurance and does not support normal signing and encryption guarantees.
results.remediation: Disable insecure guest logons and provide authenticated access to file services.

results.title: Remote Desktop requires Network Level Authentication
results.message: Remote Desktop does not require Network Level Authentication.
results.rationale: NLA reduces pre-authentication attack surface and resource consumption.
results.remediation: Enable "Require user authentication for remote connections by using Network Level Authentication."

results.title: WinRM Basic authentication is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Basic authentication relies on transport protection and is unnecessary in most domain-managed environments.
results.remediation: Disable Basic authentication for WinRM client and service. Use Kerberos, certificate authentication, or another approved mechanism.

results.title: WinRM unencrypted traffic is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Allowing unencrypted WinRM traffic can expose management data and credentials.
results.remediation: Disable unencrypted WinRM traffic and use HTTPS or message-level protection provided by supported authentication protocols.

results.title: LLMNR is disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: LLMNR can enable name-resolution poisoning and credential relay when DNS resolution fails.
results.remediation: Enable "Turn off multicast name resolution" after validating name-resolution dependencies.

results.title: Remote Registry service is disabled
results.message: Remote Registry is configured for automatic startup and is currently running.
results.rationale: Remote Registry expands the remote administration surface and is unnecessary for many systems.
results.remediation: Disable the Remote Registry service unless a documented management dependency exists.

results.title: LAN Manager authentication level refuses LM and NTLMv1
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: LM and NTLMv1 provide materially weaker authentication than NTLMv2 or Kerberos.
results.remediation: Set "Network security: LAN Manager authentication level" to "Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM" after auditing legacy dependencies.

results.title: Microsoft Defender real-time protection is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Real-time protection inspects activity as files and processes are accessed.
results.remediation: Enable real-time protection or document an approved third-party endpoint protection exception.

results.title: Cloud-delivered protection is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Cloud-delivered protection improves detection of new and rapidly changing threats.
results.remediation: Enable cloud-delivered protection at the organization-approved membership level.

results.title: Behavior monitoring is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Behavior monitoring detects suspicious runtime activity that static signatures can miss.
results.remediation: Enable behavior monitoring in Microsoft Defender Antivirus policy.

results.title: Downloaded files and attachments are scanned
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Scanning downloaded files and attachments reduces exposure from common delivery channels.
results.remediation: Enable IOAV protection in Microsoft Defender Antivirus policy.

results.title: Automatic sample submission is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Sample submission improves cloud analysis and response time for unknown files.
results.remediation: Configure sample submission according to organizational privacy requirements; avoid Never Send unless formally accepted.

results.title: Potentially unwanted application protection is enabled
results.message: Potentially unwanted application protection is enabled in Block mode.
results.rationale: PUA blocking reduces adware, bundlers, and software that weakens device security.
results.remediation: Set potentially unwanted application protection to Block.

results.title: Microsoft Defender tamper protection is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Tamper protection helps prevent unauthorized changes to core endpoint protection settings.
results.remediation: Enable tamper protection through the Microsoft Defender portal or supported management channel.

results.title: Defender signatures are recent
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Stale signatures reduce detection coverage, particularly when cloud protection is unavailable.
results.remediation: Restore update connectivity and investigate update channel, proxy, or platform health issues.

results.title: Defender network protection is enabled
results.message: Defender network protection is configured in Audit mode rather than Block mode.
results.rationale: Network protection blocks connections to malicious or low-reputation destinations from supported processes.
results.remediation: Deploy network protection in audit mode first, review impact, then enable block mode.

results.title: Core Attack Surface Reduction rules are enforced
results.expected: 5 required rules in approved enforcement modes
results.message: One required ASR rule remains in Audit mode.

evidence.Name: Block Office applications from creating child processes

results.rationale: ASR rules disrupt common malware behaviors such as credential theft, malicious Office child processes, and ransomware activity.
results.remediation: Pilot non-standard rules in Audit mode, tune legitimate impact, and move approved rules to Block or Warn. Avoid broad exclusions.

results.title: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled
results.message: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is pending disablement and requires a restart.
results.rationale: PowerShell 2.0 lacks modern logging and security capabilities and can be abused for downgrade attacks.
results.remediation: Remove the Windows PowerShell 2.0 optional feature after validating legacy script compatibility.

results.title: PowerShell Script Block Logging is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Script Block Logging records de-obfuscated PowerShell content and provides high-value investigation telemetry.
results.remediation: Enable "Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging." Protect and forward the PowerShell Operational log.

results.title: PowerShell Module Logging is enabled
results.expected: Enabled with at least one module pattern
results.message: PowerShell Module Logging is not enabled through machine policy.
results.rationale: Module logging adds pipeline and command visibility that complements Script Block Logging.
results.remediation: Enable PowerShell Module Logging and configure approved module patterns, commonly "*" for broad visibility after sizing log collection.

results.title: Process creation events include command lines
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Command-line context substantially improves investigation and detection quality for process creation events.
results.remediation: Enable "Include command line in process creation events." Restrict Security log access because arguments can contain sensitive data.

results.title: Credential Validation auditing captures success and failure
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Credential validation events provide visibility into authentication attempts and failures.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Logon > Credential Validation to Success and Failure.

results.title: Security Group Management auditing captures success
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Changes to privileged and access-control groups are high-value identity events.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Management > Security Group Management to include Success.

results.title: User Account Management auditing captures success and failure
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: User account creation, deletion, enablement, and password changes require reliable audit visibility.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Management > User Account Management to Success and Failure.

results.title: Process Creation auditing captures success
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Process creation telemetry is foundational for Windows detection engineering and incident response.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Detailed Tracking > Process Creation to include Success.

results.title: Logon auditing captures success and failure
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Successful and failed logon events support authentication monitoring, investigation, and anomaly detection.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Logon/Logoff > Logon to Success and Failure.

results.title: Special Logon auditing captures success
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Special logon events identify sessions receiving administrator-equivalent privileges.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Logon/Logoff > Special Logon to include Success.

results.title: Audit Policy Change auditing captures success
results.message: AuditQuerySystemPolicy returned access denied while reading Audit Policy Change.
results.rationale: Changes to audit policy can reduce visibility and should themselves be observable.
results.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Policy Change > Audit Policy Change to include Success.

results.title: Security event log has sufficient capacity
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Undersized logs can overwrite evidence before it is collected or investigated.
results.remediation: Increase the Security log maximum size and forward relevant events to centralized storage.

results.title: System event log has sufficient capacity
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Adequate retention supports troubleshooting and incident reconstruction.
results.remediation: Increase the System log maximum size based on event volume and forwarding latency.

results.title: PowerShell Operational log has sufficient capacity
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: PowerShell telemetry can be high volume and valuable evidence can be lost if the log is too small.
results.remediation: Enable the PowerShell Operational log and increase its maximum size based on script activity and forwarding latency.

results.title: Operating system volume is protected by BitLocker
results.message: The operating system volume is not protected by BitLocker.
results.rationale: Full-volume encryption protects data at rest when storage media or devices are lost or removed.
results.remediation: Enable BitLocker using an approved protector and escrow recovery information in the organization-approved directory.

results.title: Secure Boot is enabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Secure Boot helps prevent untrusted boot components from loading before Windows.
results.remediation: Enable UEFI Secure Boot after validating firmware, operating system, and virtualization support.

results.title: AutoRun and AutoPlay are disabled
results.message: The effective state matches the selected baseline.
results.rationale: Automatic execution from removable media increases malware delivery risk.
results.remediation: Disable AutoPlay for all drives and disable AutoRun commands through Group Policy or Intune.

results.title: Solicited Remote Assistance is disabled
results.message: Solicited Remote Assistance is enabled for the approved support workflow.
results.rationale: Remote Assistance adds an interactive remote-access path that should be explicitly justified.
results.remediation: Disable "Configure Solicited Remote Assistance" unless the support model requires it; use a documented exception when enabled.

exception.reason: The approved support workflow requires solicited Remote Assistance on this managed server group.
compensatingControls: Access is restricted to the support group.
Session activity is logged and reviewed.
